page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each of the 64 vCPUs corresponds to a dedicated physical core, ensuring consistent compute performance without resource sharing at the thread level.

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 28 Gbps, facilitating reliable data transfer rates for distributed environments.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and balanced resource density position this instance as a stable platform for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a streamlined, cost-effective option for compute-and-memory-balanced tasks where local high-speed scratch space or hardware acceleration is unnecessary.

Suitable workloads for the ecs.g9i.16xlarge include enterprise application hosting, web serving, mid-sized databases, containerized microservices, and general-purpose business applications that benefit from dedicated Intel Xeon processing power and a balanced memory footprint.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual instance configured with 64 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, powered by 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it features a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, ensuring dedicated compute resources per vCPU. The instance provides 256.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 28 Gbps. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-balanced workloads by avoiding the premium of specialized hardware accelerators or bundled local disks. It is well-suited for enterprise application hosting, containerized microservices, web serving, and mid-sized database management.

256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
